name: prompt-declaration-language

metadata.name: prompt-declaration-language
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License

documentation: # PDL (Prompt Declaration Language) [![tests](https://github.com/IBM/prompt-declaration-language/actions/workflows/build.yml/badge.svg?branch=main)](https://github.com/IBM/prompt-declaration-language/actions?query=workflow%3Abuild+branch%3Amain) [![night tests](https://github.com/IBM/prompt-declaration-language/actions/workflows/run-examples.yml/badge.svg?branch=main)](https://github.com/IBM/prompt-declaration-language/actions?query=workflow%3Arun-examples+branch%3Amain) [![documentation](https://github.com/IBM/prompt-declaration-language/actions/workflows/mkdocs-gh-pages.yml/badge.svg?branch=main)](https://github.com/IBM/prompt-declaration-language/actions?query=workflow%3Amkdocs-gh-pages+branch%3Amain) [![PyPI version shields.io](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/prompt-declaration-language?color=success)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/prompt-declaration-language/) [![Quay Publish](https://github.com/IBM/prompt-declaration-language/actions/workflows/publish-quay.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/IBM/prompt-declaration-language/actions/workflows/publish-quay.yaml) [![Imports: isort](https://img.shields.io/badge/%20imports-isort-%231674b1?style=flat&labelColor=ef8336)](https://pycqa.github.io/isort/) [![Code style: black](https://img.shields.io/badge/code%20style-black-000000.svg)](https://github.com/psf/black) [![linting: pylint](https://img.shields.io/badge/linting-pylint-yellowgreen)](https://github.com/PyCQA/pylint) [![security: bandit](https://img.shields.io/badge/security-bandit-yellow.svg)](https://github.com/PyCQA/bandit) [![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache%202.0-blue.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0) [![CII Best Practices](https://bestpractices.coreinfrastructure.org/projects/9672/badge)](https://bestpractices.coreinfrastructure.org/projects/9672) ## Overview PDL is a declarative language designed for developers to create reliable, composable LLM prompts and integrate them into software systems. It provides a structured way to specify prompt templates, enforce validation, and compose LLM calls with traditional rule-based systems. ### Basic LLM Call Minimum installation. ```bash pip install prompt-declaration-language ``` See [here](https://ibm.github.io/prompt-declaration-language/tutorial/#using-ollama-models) for instructions on how to install an Ollama model locally. You can create a PDL file (YAML format): ```yaml description: Simple LLM interaction text: - "write a hello world example\n" - model: ollama/granite-code:8b parameters: stop_sequences: '!' temperature: 0 ``` and run it: ```bash pdl ``` ## Key Features - **LLM Integration**: Compatible with any LLM, including IBM watsonx - **Prompt Engineering**: - Template system for single/multi-shot prompting - Composition of multiple LLM calls - Integration with tools (code execution & APIs) - **Development Tools**: - Type checking for model I/O - Python SDK - Chat API support - Live document visualization for debugging - **Control Flow**: Variables, conditionals, loops, and functions - **I/O Operations**: File/stdin reading, JSON parsing - **API Integration**: Native REST API support (Python) ## Documentation - [Documenation](https://ibm.github.io/prompt-declaration-language/) - [API References](https://ibm.github.io/prompt-declaration-language/api_reference/) - [Tutorial](https://ibm.github.io/prompt-declaration-language/tutorial/) ### Quick Reference ## Quick Start Guide ### Installation Requires Python 3.11+ (Windows users should use WSL) ```bash # Basic installation pip install prompt-declaration-language # Development installation with examples pip install 'prompt-declaration-language[examples]' ``` ### Environment Setup You can run PDL with LLM models in local using [Ollama](https://ollama.com), or other cloud service. If you use WatsonX: ```bash export WATSONX_URL="https://{region}.ml.cloud.ibm.com" export WATSONX_APIKEY="your-api-key" export WATSONX_PROJECT_ID="your-project-id" ``` If you use Replicate: ```bash export REPLICATE_API_TOKEN="your-token" ``` ### IDE Configuration VSCode setup for syntax highlighting and validation: ```json // .vscode/settings.json { "yaml.schemas": { "https://ibm.github.io/prompt-declaration-language/dist/pdl-schema.json": "*.pdl" }, "files.associations": { "*.pdl": "yaml", } } ``` ## Code Examples ### Variable Definition & Template Usage In this example we use external content imput.yaml and watonsx as a LLM provider. ```yaml description: Template with variables defs: USER_INPUT: read: ../examples/code/data.yaml parser: yaml text: - model: watsonx/ibm/granite-34b-code-instruct input: | Process this input: ${USER_INPUT} Format the output as JSON. ``` ### Python Code Integration ```yaml description: Code execution example text: - "\nFind a random number between 1 and 20\n" - def: N lang: python code: | import random result = random.randint(1, 20) - "\nthe result is (${ N })\n" ``` ### Chat chat interactions: ```yaml description: chatbot text: - read: def: user_input message: "hi? [/bye to exit]\n" contribute: [context] - repeat: text: - model: ollama/granite-code:8b - read: def: user_input message: "> " contribute: [context] until: ${ user_input == '/bye'} ``` ## Debugging Tools ### Log Inspection ```bash pdl --log ``` ### Trace Generation and Live Document Visualization ```bash pdl --trace ``` Upload trace files to the [Live Document Viewer](https://ibm.github.io/prompt-declaration-language/viewer/) for visual debugging. ## Contributing See the [contribution guidelines](https://ibm.github.io/prompt-declaration-language/contrib) for details on: - Code style - Testing requirements - PR process - Issue reporting ## References - [PDL Schema](https://github.com/IBM/prompt-declaration-language/blob/main/src/pdl/pdl-schema.json) - [arXiv Paper](http://arxiv.org/abs/2410.19135)

functions.name: pdl.pdl.exec_program
documentation:
Execute a PDL program given as a value of type `pdl.pdl_ast.Program`.

Args:
    prog: Program to execute.
    config: Interpreter configuration. Defaults to None.
    scope: Environment defining the initial variables in scope to execute the program. Defaults to None.
    loc: Source code location mapping. Defaults to None.
    output: Configure the output of the returned value of this function. Defaults to `"result"`

Returns:
    Return the final result if `output` is set to `"result"`. If set of `all`, it returns a dictionary containing, `result`, `scope`, and `trace`.

functions.name: pdl.pdl.exec_dict
documentation:
Execute a PDL program given as a dictionary.

Args:
    prog: Program to execute.
    config: Interpreter configuration. Defaults to None.
    scope: Environment defining the initial variables in scope to execute the program. Defaults to None.
    loc: Source code location mapping. Defaults to None.
    output: Configure the output of the returned value of this function. Defaults to `"result"`

Returns:
    Return the final result.

functions.name: pdl.pdl.exec_str
documentation:
Execute a PDL program given as YAML string.

Args:
    prog: Program to execute.
    config: Interpreter configuration. Defaults to None.
    scope: Environment defining the initial variables in scope to execute the program. Defaults to None.
    output: Configure the output of the returned value of this function. Defaults to `"result"`

Returns:
    Return the final result.

functions.name: pdl.pdl.exec_file
documentation:
Execute a PDL program given as YAML file.

Args:
    prog: Program to execute.
    config: Interpreter configuration. Defaults to None.
    scope: Environment defining the initial variables in scope to execute the program. Defaults to None.
    output: Configure the output of the returned value of this function. Defaults to `"result"`

Returns:
    Return the final result.

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl.InterpreterConfig.cwd

Configuration parameters of the PDL interpreter.

modules.name: pdl._version

modules.name: pdl.pdl_ast
documentation: PDL programs are represented by the Pydantic data structure defined in this file.

classes.name: pdl.pdl_ast.Block

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.Block.spec
documentation: Type specification of the result of the block.

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.Block.defs
documentation: Set of definitions executed before the execution of the block.

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.Block.assign
documentation: Name of the variable used to store the result of the execution of the block.

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.Block.contribute
documentation: Indicate if the block contributes to the result and background context.

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.Block.parser
documentation: Parser to use to construct a value out of a string result.

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.Block.fallback
documentation: Block to execute in case of error.

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.Block.role
documentation: Role associated to the block and sub-blocks.
Common fields for all PDL blocks.

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.CallBlock.args
documentation: Arguments of the function with their values.

classes.name: pdl.pdl_ast.LitellmParameters

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.LitellmParameters.timeout
documentation: Timeout in seconds for completion requests (Defaults to 600 seconds).

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.LitellmParameters.temperature
documentation: The temperature parameter for controlling the randomness of the output (default is 1.0).

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.LitellmParameters.top_p
documentation: The top-p parameter for nucleus sampling (default is 1.0).

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.LitellmParameters.n
documentation: The number of completions to generate (default is 1).

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.LitellmParameters.stop
documentation: Up to 4 sequences where the LLM API will stop generating further tokens.

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.LitellmParameters.max_tokens
documentation: The maximum number of tokens in the generated completion (default is infinity).

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.LitellmParameters.presence_penalty
documentation: It is used to penalize new tokens based on their existence in the text so far.

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.LitellmParameters.frequency_penalty
documentation: It is used to penalize new tokens based on their frequency in the text so far.

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.LitellmParameters.logit_bias
documentation: Used to modify the probability of specific tokens appearing in the completion.

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.LitellmParameters.user
documentation: A unique identifier representing your end-user. This can help the LLM provider to monitor and detect abuse.

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.LitellmParameters.logprobs
documentation: Whether to return log probabilities of the output tokens or not. If true, returns the log probabilities of each output token returned in the content of message

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.LitellmParameters.top_logprobs
documentation: top_logprobs (int, optional): An integer between 0 and 5 specifying the number of most likely tokens to return at each token position, each with an associated log probability. logprobs must be set to true if this parameter is used.

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.LitellmParameters.extra_headers
documentation: Additional headers to include in the request.

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.LitellmParameters.functions
documentation: A list of functions to apply to the conversation messages (default is an empty list)

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.LitellmParameters.function_call
documentation: The name of the function to call within the conversation (default is an empty string)

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.LitellmParameters.base_url
documentation: Base URL for the API (default is None).

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.LitellmParameters.model_list
documentation: List of api base, version, keys.
pass in a list of api_base,keys, etc.

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.LitellmParameters.mock_response
documentation: If provided, return a mock completion response for testing or debugging purposes (default is None).

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.LitellmParameters.custom_llm_provider
documentation: Used for Non-OpenAI LLMs, Example usage for bedrock, set model="amazon.titan-tg1-large" and custom_llm_provider="bedrock"

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.LitellmParameters.max_retries
documentation: The number of retries to attempt (default is 0).
Parameters passed to LiteLLM. More details at https://docs.litellm.ai/docs/completion/input.

classes.name: pdl.pdl_ast.LitellmModelBlock
documentation: Call a LLM through the LiteLLM API: https://docs.litellm.ai/.

classes.name: pdl.pdl_ast.GetBlock

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.GetBlock.get
documentation: Name of the variable to access.
Get the value of a variable.

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.DataBlock.raw
documentation: Do not evaluate expressions inside strings.

classes.name: pdl.pdl_ast.TextBlock
documentation: Create the concatenation of the stringify version of the result of each block of the list of blocks.

classes.name: pdl.pdl_ast.LastOfBlock
documentation: Return the value of the last block if the list of blocks.

classes.name: pdl.pdl_ast.ArrayBlock
documentation: Return the array of values computed by each block of the list of blocks.

classes.name: pdl.pdl_ast.ObjectBlock
documentation: Return the object where the value of each field is defined by a block. If the body of the object is an array, the resulting object is the union of the objects computed by each element of the array.

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.MessageBlock.role
documentation: Role of associated to the message.

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.IfBlock.then
documentation: Branch to exectute if the condition is true.

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.IfBlock.elses
documentation: Branch to execute if the condition is false.

classes.name: pdl.pdl_ast.JoinConfig
documentation: Configure how loop iterations should be combined.

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.JoinText.iteration_type
documentation: String concatenation of the result of each iteration.

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.JoinText.join_string
documentation: String used to concatenate each iteration of the loop.

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.JoinArray.iteration_type
documentation: Return the result of each iteration as an array.

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.JoinLastOf.iteration_type
documentation: Return the result of the last iteration.

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.ForBlock.join
documentation: Define how to combine the result of each iteration.

classes.name: pdl.pdl_ast.RepeatBlock

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.RepeatBlock.join
documentation: Define how to combine the result of each iteration.
Repeat the execution of a block for a fixed number of iterations.

classes.name: pdl.pdl_ast.RepeatUntilBlock

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.RepeatUntilBlock.join
documentation: Define how to combine the result of each iteration.
Repeat the execution of a block until a condition is satisfied.

classes.name: pdl.pdl_ast.ReadBlock

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.ReadBlock.read
documentation: Name of the file to read. If `None`, read the standard input.

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.ReadBlock.message
documentation: Message to prompt the user to enter a value.

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.ReadBlock.multiline
documentation: Indicate if one or multiple lines shoud be read.
Read from a file or standard input.

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.IncludeBlock.include
documentation: Name of the file to include.

classes.name: pdl.pdl_ast.EmptyBlock
documentation: Block without an action. It can contain definitions.

class_variables.name: pdl.pdl_ast.Program.root
documentation: Entry point to parse a PDL program using Pydantic.

functions.name: pdl.pdl_interpreter.generate
documentation:
Execute the PDL program defined in `pdl_file`.

Args:
    pdl_file: Program to execute.
    log_file: File where the log is written. If `None`, use `log.txt`.
    initial_scope: Environment defining the variables in scope to execute the program.
    state: Initial state of the interpreter.
    trace_file: Indicate if the execution trace must be produced and the file to save it.

functions.name: pdl.pdl_interpreter.write_trace
documentation:
Write the execution trace into a file.

Args:
    trace_file:  File to save the execution trace.
    trace: Execution trace.

functions.name: pdl.pdl_interpreter.process_prog
documentation:
Execute a PDL program.

Args:
    state: Initial state of the interpreter.
    scope: Environment defining the variables in scope to execute the program.
    prog: Program to execute.
    loc: Source code location mapping. Defaults to empty_block_location.

Returns:
    Return the final result, the background messages, the final variable mapping, and the execution trace.

Raises:
    PDLRuntimeError: If the program raises an error.

functions.name: pdl.pdl_interpreter.step_block
documentation: for result, document, scope, trace in schedule(doc_generators):
